# Live and Grieve‚ĄĘ ‚ÄĒ Tri-Pillars‚ĄĘ LLC

> Live and Grieve‚ĄĘ is a structured, peer-facilitated grief education program for adults and youth. It is not therapy. It is community-based grief support grounded in five peer-reviewed research frameworks and the Wolfelt companioning philosophical influence, designed to be delivered by trained facilitators in hospitals, hospice organizations, churches, nonprofits, and corporate/EAP settings.

## What This Site Is

tripillarstudio.com is the primary platform for Tri-Pillars‚ĄĘ LLC, the organization behind Live and Grieve‚ĄĘ and Live and Grieve Youth‚ĄĘ (LGY). The site serves prospective facilitators, institutional partners, and individuals seeking grief support resources.

## The Program

**Live and Grieve‚ĄĘ** is a 52-week peer-facilitated grief education program structured in 6 modules of 10 lessons each (60 lessons total), designed to be delivered in weekly group sessions across 13 weeks per cohort.

- **Not therapy.** No clinical license required to facilitate.
- **Facilitators** are trained companions and witnesses ‚ÄĒ not experts or fixers.
- **3 certification tracks:** Community, Professional, and Ministry.
- **Formats:** Group cohort (in-person or virtual), Solo Companion (self-paced individual).

**Live and Grieve Youth‚ĄĘ (LGY)** is the youth-focused parallel program for adolescents navigating grief.

## Research Frameworks

Live and Grieve‚ĄĘ is grounded in five peer-reviewed research frameworks in two layers, plus Wolfelt's Companioning as a philosophical influence (not a peer-reviewed framework):

**Layer 1 ‚ÄĒ Theoretical Foundation:**
1. **Dual Process Model** (Stroebe & Schut, 1999) ‚ÄĒ oscillation between loss-orientation and restoration-orientation
2. **Worden's Tasks of Mourning** (J. William Worden) ‚ÄĒ four active tasks of grief work
3. **Continuing Bonds Theory** (Klass, Silverman & Nickman, 1996) ‚ÄĒ maintaining connection with the deceased

**Layer 2 ‚ÄĒ Applied Practice:**
4. **Meaning Reconstruction** (Robert Neimeyer) ‚ÄĒ rebuilding a world of meaning after loss
5. **Self-Compassion** (Kristin Neff) ‚ÄĒ applying self-compassion practices to grief
**Philosophical Influence ‚ÄĒ Wolfelt's Companioning** (Alan Wolfelt) ‚ÄĒ witnessing grief rather than fixing it (philosophical influence, not a peer-reviewed framework)

## Solo Companion

The Solo Companion is a self-paced digital version of the Live and Grieve‚ĄĘ adult program for individuals who cannot join a group cohort. Available at https://solo.tripillarstudio.com. Pricing: $24.99 one-time or 3√ó$9.99.

## Hampshire County Pilot

Live and Grieve‚ĄĘ is launching its first institutional pilot in Hampshire County, West Virginia in May 2026 through a formal Institutional License Agreement (ILA). This is the first real-world cohort deployment of the program.

## Organization

**Tri-Pillars‚ĄĘ LLC** was founded by Wayne and Jamie Simms following the loss of their son Jacoby and nephew Ian. The organization is Kentucky-based with national reach.

- Contact: wayne@tripillarstudio.com / jamie@tripillarstudio.com
- YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/@liveandgrieve_3
- Phone: (270) 302-8814

## Important Distinctions

- Live and Grieve‚ĄĘ is grief **education**, not grief **therapy**
- Facilitators are companions and witnesses ‚ÄĒ not clinicians or counselors
- The program does not claim clinical outcomes
- No evidence-based claims are made about Live and Grieve‚ĄĘ or LGY
